prompt: The annotation is not visible at runtime via reflection. What is the bug?
choices: Custom annotations are ignored by the JVM unless the interface is public
Without @Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME) the annotation is discarded by the compiler and is not available via reflection at runtime
Annotation values must be accessed via getAnnotation(), not isAnnotationPresent()
explanation: The default retention policy is RetentionPolicy.CLASS, which writes the annotation to the .class file but does not preserve it at runtime. Reflection APIs read runtime metadata, so they cannot see CLASS-retained annotations. Fix: add @Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME) above @interface Tag.
